title: Accounts Receivable Aging and Cash Collection
dataSources: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices"` (invoice_id, customer, amount, invoice_date, due_date, payment_date, status)
spl:
index=business sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices" status="open" earliest=-1y
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_overdue=round((now()-due_epoch)/86400, 0)
| eval aging_bucket=case(
    days_overdue <= 0, "Current",
    days_overdue <= 30, "1-30 days",
    days_overdue <= 60, "31-60 days",
    days_overdue <= 90, "61-90 days",
    1=1, "90+ days")
| stats sum(amount) as total_outstanding, dc(invoice_id) as invoice_count, dc(customer) as customers by aging_bucket
| eventstats sum(total_outstanding) as grand_total
| eval pct_of_total=round(100*total_outstanding/grand_total, 1)
| sort aging_bucket
| table aging_bucket, invoice_count, customers, total_outstanding, pct_of_total
description: Shows outstanding receivables by aging bucket — current, 30-day, 60-day, 90-day, 120+ day — with customer-level drill-down. CFOs and controllers see cash collection health at a glance, identify customers drifting into bad debt territory, and measure Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) against targets. Early visibility enables proactive collection before debts become uncollectable.
value: Shows outstanding receivables by aging bucket — current, 30-day, 60-day, 90-day, 120+ day — with customer-level drill-down. CFOs and controllers see cash collection health at a glance, identify customers drifting into bad debt territory, and measure Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) against targets. Early visibility enables proactive collection before debts become uncollectable.
implementation: (1) Import open AR invoices via DB Connect from ERP; (2) schedule daily; (3) alert collections team when any customer exceeds 60 days overdue; (4) calculate DSO: (AR balance / revenue) × days in period; (5) trend DSO monthly for CFO reporting; (6) segment by customer segment and region for targeted collection strategies.
visualization: Stacked bar (AR by aging bucket), Table (top overdue customers), Single value (total overdue, DSO), Line chart (DSO trend).
knownFalsePositives: Aging shifts at period close, when payment terms change, during large disputes, or with working-capital and collection programmes in flight.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices"` (invoice_id, customer, amount, invoice_date, due_date, payment_date, status).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Import open AR invoices via DB Connect from ERP; (2) schedule daily; (3) alert collections team when any customer exceeds 60 days overdue; (4) calculate DSO: (AR balance / revenue) × days in period; (5) trend DSO monthly for CFO reporting; (6) segment by customer segment and region for targeted collection strategies.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices" status="open" earliest=-1y
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_overdue=round((now()-due_epoch)/86400, 0)
| eval aging_bucket=case(
    days_overdue <= 0, "Current",
    days_overdue <= 30, "1-30 days",
    days_overdue <= 60, "31-60 days",
    days_overdue <= 90, "61-90 days",
    1=1, "90+ days")
| stats sum(amount) as total_outstanding, dc(invoice_id) as invoice_count, dc(customer) as customers by aging_bucket
| eventstats sum(total_outstanding) as grand_total
| eval pct_of_total=round(100*total_outstanding/grand_total, 1)
| sort aging_bucket
| table aging_bucket, invoice_count, customers, total_outstanding, pct_of_total
```

Understanding this SPL

**Accounts Receivable Aging and Cash Collection** — Shows outstanding receivables by aging bucket — current, 30-day, 60-day, 90-day, 120+ day — with customer-level drill-down. CFOs and controllers see cash collection health at a glance, identify customers drifting into bad debt territory, and measure Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) against targets. Early visibility enables proactive collection before debts become uncollectable.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices"` (invoice_id, customer, amount, invoice_date, due_date, payment_date, status). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: dbx:ar_invoices. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_overdue** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **aging_bucket** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by aging_bucket** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pct_of_total** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Accounts Receivable Aging and Cash Collection**): table aging_bucket, invoice_count, customers, total_outstanding, pct_of_total

Step 3 — Validate
Match AR aging totals to the ERP or finance subledger and the same accounting close. Reconcile a sample of open invoices and DSO math to the controller’s pack.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (AR by aging bucket), Table (top overdue customers), Single value (total overdue, DSO), Line chart (DSO trend).
grandmaExplanation: We watch receivables aging and collection so cash timing stays visible and problem accounts do not hide in the roll-up.
